name: Namtar
description: Namtar, whose name translates to 'fate' or 'destiny,' is a minor Sumerian deity who serves as the vizier or minister of the underworld goddess Ereshkigal. He is considered a demon or divine servant responsible for dispensing diseases, plagues, and other misfortunes upon humanity, acting as Ereshkigal's messenger and executor of her decrees. Namtar is often depicted as a powerful and fearsome figure, embodying the harsh realities of mortality and the inevitability of suffering. While not inherently malevolent, he represents the darker aspects of fate and the consequences of divine judgment. His role in Sumerian mythology highlights the Sumerian understanding of the world as a place where both blessings and curses originate from the divine realm. He is also connected to the spread of illnesses and epidemics, a reflection of the Sumerians' anxieties about disease and death. Namtar's power stems directly from Ereshkigal, and he is essentially an extension of her will. He carries out her commands without question, delivering suffering and death to those she deems worthy of punishment. He is a constant reminder of the power of the underworld and the vulnerability of human life.
subtitle: Fate Bringer of Disease and Death

keyMyths.title: Inanna's Descent to the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Namtar afflicts Inanna with various diseases as she passes through the gates of the underworld, as dictated by Ereshkigal. This demonstrates his role as an agent of the underworld goddess's power.

keyMyths.title: Namtar's role as Ereshkigal's Vizier
keyMyths.description: Namtar is the chief minister and messenger of Ereshkigal. He carries out her orders to inflict curses and diseases on the living.
keyMyths.source: Sumerian Mythology: A Study of Spiritual and Literary Achievement in the Third Millennium B.C.

keyMyths.title: Dispensing of Disease
keyMyths.description: Namtar is responsible for releasing 60 diseases upon the earth, acting as the distributor of pestilence. This further cements his association with suffering.
keyMyths.source: Myths from Mesopotamia: Creation, The Flood, Gilgamesh, and Others

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: Sumerian Mythology: A Study of Spiritual and Literary Achievement in the Third Millennium B.C.
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of Sumerian myths and religious beliefs.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive overview of Sumerian myths and religious beliefs.

sources.author: Stephanie Dalley
sources.work: Myths from Mesopotamia: Creation, The Flood, Gilgamesh, and Others
sources.description: Offers translations and interpretations of various Mesopotamian myths, including those involving Namtar.
